title: Data standards and validation
text: One row represents one respondent-item response within one assessment. required columns respondent_id, item_id, direction, effect. Recommended metadata include project, site, team, role, timepoint, assessment date. Direction coded 1 = potential barrier, 2 = neutral, 3 = potential facilitator. Effect coded 0 = weak/effect 1 = strong effect. Leave effect missing direction neutral.

title: Analyze and Visualize Pragmatic Context Assessment Tool Data
text: pcatR independent R package reproducible analysis 14-item Pragmatic Context Assessment Tool (pCAT). imports validates response data, classifies barriers facilitators, summarizes item-level patterns, describes team agreement disagreement, compares repeated assessments, creates implementation action-planning tables, produces publication-ready figures.

title: Analyze and Visualize Pragmatic Context Assessment Tool Data
text: pCAT developed brief pragmatic reflection problem-solving tool, conventional multi-item psychometric scale. Accordingly, pcatR: reports items constructs rather single overall score; calculate endorse validated total pCAT scale score; uses -2 +2 display code plotting within-item transition descriptions; treats implementation-strategy links prompts local deliberation, automated prescriptions; replace fuller contextual assessment comprehensive CFIR determinant evaluation required. Read Technical User Guide analyzing study operational data. installation, open packaged guide :
code: pcat_user_guide()

title: Analyze and Visualize Pragmatic Context Assessment Tool Data
text: export directory contains manifest, validation findings, classified responses, item summaries, consensus diagnostics, action-plan worksheet, optional multi-page profile PDF. Set include_classified = FALSE respondent-level export required.
code: library(pcatR)  # Use synthetic example data, or replace this with pcat_read_csv("file.csv"). dat <- pcat_example_data()  analysis <- pcat_analyse(   dat,   group_vars = c("site_id", "timepoint"),   require_complete = TRUE,   validation_action = "none" )  analysis pcat_validation_issues(analysis$validation) head(analysis$summary) head(analysis$consensus) head(analysis$action_plan)  plot_pcat_profile(   analysis$classified,   group_vars = c("site_id", "timepoint"),   label = "cfir_original_construct" )  pcat_write_analysis(   analysis,   path = "pcat_analysis_outputs",   overwrite = TRUE )

code: pcat_analyse(   data,   group_vars = NULL,   respondent_id = "respondent_id",   key_cols = NULL,   require_complete = FALSE,   neutral_effect = c("flag", "allow", "set_missing"),   validation_action = c("none", "warn", "error"),   strict = FALSE,   suppress_below = NULL,   agreement_threshold = 0.6,   polarization_min = 0.2,   minimum_n = 2L,   include_action_plan = TRUE,   barrier_threshold = 0.5,   strong_barrier_threshold = 0.2,   include_approximate = FALSE )  # S3 method for class 'pcat_analysis' print(x, ...)

title: Run the standard pCAT analysis workflow — pcat_analyse
text: data Long-format pCAT response data. group_vars Optional character vector grouping columns, site_id timepoint. respondent_id Name respondent identifier column. key_cols Columns defining one assessment validation. omitted, available project, site, team, timepoint, assessment date, respondent identifiers used. require_complete Logical; require exactly one response every pCAT item 1–14 within assessment. neutral_effect handle neutral direction paired recorded effect: flag , allow , set effect missing. validation_action Whether validation findings generate condition, warning, error. strict Logical; treat warnings invalid setting validation object's valid field. suppress_below Optional positive minimum respondent count. Numeric summary results threshold replaced missing values. agreement_threshold Minimum dominant-side share used consensus label. polarization_min Minimum barrier facilitator shares required label item polarized. minimum_n Minimum number valid direction responses required consensus label. include_action_plan Logical; create barrier-focused action-plan table. barrier_threshold Minimum barrier prevalence action-plan inclusion. strong_barrier_threshold Minimum strong-barrier prevalence action-plan inclusion. include_approximate Logical; include source table's non-identical Relative Priority Relative Advantage approximation. x pcat_analysis object. ... Additional arguments, currently ignored print method.

title: Instrument metadata, CFIR mappings, and included example data — pcat_items

title: Instrument metadata, CFIR mappings, and included example data — pcat_items
text: cfir_version Mapping version return: source versions, original CFIR, updated 2022 CFIR. include_secondary Logical; retain secondary item--construct links. 2026 supplementary mapping gives item 10 secondary Funding link. construct Optional original-CFIR source-table construct name used filter candidate strategies. tier Candidate strategy endorsement tier: , tier 1, tier 2. include_approximate Logical; include source table's non-identical Relative Priority Relative Advantage approximation.

title: Instrument metadata, CFIR mappings, and included example data — pcat_items
text: pcat_items() keeps one primary updated-CFIR construct per item item-level reporting. pcat_construct_map() exposes every retained item-construct link. Candidate strategies prompts local deliberation, automatically selected validated prescriptions.

title: Instrument metadata, CFIR mappings, and included example data — pcat_items
text: data frame. pcat_items() one row per pCAT item; pcat_construct_map() one row per item-construct link.

what: Prepare, import, and reshape pCAT data — pcat_standardize
title: Prepare, import, and reshape pCAT data — pcat_standardize
text: Reads standard pCAT CSV files, standardizes user column names, creates entry templates, writes CSV templates, converts responses long wide layouts.

title: Prepare, import, and reshape pCAT data — pcat_standardize
code: pcat_read_csv(   path,   layout = c("auto", "long", "wide"),   item_prefix = "item",   na = c("", "NA") )  pcat_standardize(   data,   respondent_id = "respondent_id",   item_id = "item_id",   direction = "direction",   effect = "effect",   project_id = NULL,   site_id = NULL,   team_id = NULL,   role = NULL,   timepoint = NULL,   assessment_date = NULL,   comment = NULL,   keep_original = TRUE )  pcat_template(   format = c("long", "wide"),   n_respondents = 1L,   include_item_text = TRUE )  pcat_write_template(   path,   format = c("long", "wide"),   n_respondents = 1L,   include_item_text = TRUE,   overwrite = FALSE )  pcat_wide_to_long(data, item_prefix = "item")  pcat_long_to_wide(data, id_cols = NULL, item_prefix = "item")

title: Prepare, import, and reshape pCAT data — pcat_standardize
text: path Input output CSV path, depending function. layout Input layout: auto-detected, long, wide. item_prefix Prefix used item numbers wide response columns. na Character values interpreted missing reading CSV. data data frame. respondent_id, item_id, direction, effect Source column names, supplied character strings, four required fields. project_id, site_id, team_id, role, timepoint, assessment_date, comment Optional source column names supplied character strings. keep_original Logical; retain source columns whose names differ standard names. format Output layout, either long wide. n_respondents Positive number blank respondent records. include_item_text Logical; include item wording long entry template. overwrite Logical; replace existing output file. id_cols Columns defining one assessment row wide output.

title: Prepare, import, and reshape pCAT data — pcat_standardize
text: Standard long data require respondent_id, item_id, direction, effect. Standard wide response names follow item01_direction, item01_effect, item 14. Free-text comments reviewed sensitive information analysis sharing.

what: Summarize, compare, and plan from pCAT data — pcat_summarise
title: Summarize, compare, and plan from pCAT data — pcat_summarise
text: Creates item-level summaries, team agreement disagreement diagnostics, paired longitudinal transitions, barrier-focused action-plan tables.

title: Summarize, compare, and plan from pCAT data — pcat_summarise
code: pcat_summarise(   data,   group_vars = NULL,   respondent_id = "respondent_id",   suppress_below = NULL )  pcat_consensus(   data,   group_vars = NULL,   agreement_threshold = 0.6,   polarization_min = 0.2,   minimum_n = 2L )  pcat_change(   data,   timepoint = "timepoint",   from,   to,   id_cols = NULL )  pcat_action_plan(   data,   group_vars = NULL,   barrier_threshold = 0.5,   strong_barrier_threshold = 0.2,   include_strategy_candidates = TRUE,   include_approximate = FALSE )

title: Summarize, compare, and plan from pCAT data — pcat_summarise
text: data Raw, validated, classified, previously summarized pCAT data, appropriate function. group_vars Optional character vector grouping columns site timepoint. respondent_id Respondent identifier column name. suppress_below Optional positive minimum respondent count. Numerical summary measures replaced missing values suppressed set TRUE. agreement_threshold Minimum dominant-side share used consensus label. polarization_min Minimum barrier facilitator shares required label item polarized. minimum_n Minimum number valid direction responses. timepoint Timepoint column name. , Earlier later timepoint values. id_cols Columns identifying paired respondent-item records. barrier_threshold Minimum barrier prevalence action-plan inclusion. strong_barrier_threshold Minimum strong-barrier prevalence action-plan inclusion. include_strategy_candidates Logical; join source-derived candidate ERIC strategies direct construct link available. include_approximate Logical; include non-identical Relative Priority Relative Advantage approximation.

title: Summarize, compare, and plan from pCAT data — pcat_summarise
text: Directional percentages (pct_barrier, pct_neutral, pct_facilitator, pct_effect_missing) use n_valid_direction. Five-category strength percentages use n_complete_class, barrier facilitator responses require nonmissing effect fully classified. Report denominators differ. Means, medians, change values based pcat_display_code strictly descriptive. Candidate strategies require local stakeholder review automatic prescriptions. Small-cell suppression applies returned summary table de-identify source classified data.

what: Locate or open the pcatR technical user guide — pcat_user_guide
title: Locate or open the pcatR technical user guide — pcat_user_guide
text: Locates technical user guide installed pcatR. guide defines package's data schema, validation rules, denominators, descriptive classification logic, privacy cautions, reporting expectations.

title: Locate or open the pcatR technical user guide — pcat_user_guide
code: pcat_user_guide(format = c("pdf", "html"), open = interactive())

title: Locate or open the pcatR technical user guide — pcat_user_guide
text: format Guide format: PDF HTML. open Logical; open guide operating system's default application.

title: Locate or open the pcatR technical user guide — pcat_user_guide
text: normalized guide path, invisibly.

title: Locate or open the pcatR technical user guide — pcat_user_guide
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ pcat_user_guide() } # }  path <- pcat_user_guide("html", open = FALSE) path #> [1] "/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/pcatR/guides/pcatR_Technical_User_Guide.html"

title: Validate pCAT response data — pcat_validate
text: Checks identifiers, item numbers, direction effect values, duplicate keys, neutral responses recorded effects, missing effects barrier facilitator directions, optional 14-item completeness.

title: Validate pCAT response data — pcat_validate
code: pcat_validate(   data,   key_cols = NULL,   require_complete = FALSE,   neutral_effect = c("flag", "allow", "set_missing"),   action = c("warn", "error", "none"),   strict = FALSE )  # S3 method for class 'pcat_validation' print(x, ...)  pcat_validation_data(x, valid_rows_only = FALSE)  pcat_validation_issues(x, level = c("all", "row", "assessment"))

title: Validate pCAT response data — pcat_validate
text: data Long-format pCAT response data. Required columns respondent_id, item_id, direction, effect. key_cols Columns defining one assessment. omitted, available project, site, team, timepoint, assessment date, respondent identifiers used. require_complete Logical; require exactly one row every item 1–14 within assessment. neutral_effect handle neutral direction paired recorded effect: flag , allow , set effect missing. action Whether validation findings generate warning, error, condition. strict Logical; treat warnings invalid setting object's valid field. x pcat_validation object. ... Additional arguments, currently ignored print method. valid_rows_only Logical; retain rows without row-level errors. level Return issues, row-level issues, assessment-completeness issues.

title: Validate pCAT response data — pcat_validate
text: default behavior flags neutral response paired either effect code warning effect strength applicable direction neutral. Validation findings remain available structured form even action = "none".

title: Validate pCAT response data — pcat_validate
text: pcat_validate() returns pcat_validation object. extractor functions return data frames. print method returns x invisibly.

title: Validate pCAT response data — pcat_validate
code: dat <- pcat_example_data() val <- pcat_validate(dat, require_complete = TRUE, action = "none") val #> <pcat_validation> #>   valid: yes #>   rows: 336 #>   errors: 0 #>   warnings: 0 #>   complete assessments required: yes head(pcat_validation_data(val)) #>   project_id respondent_id site_id      role timepoint assessment_date item_id #> 1  PCAT_DEMO          R001  SITE_A clinician  planning      2026-01-15       1 #> 2  PCAT_DEMO          R001  SITE_A clinician  planning      2026-01-15       2 #> 3  PCAT_DEMO          R001  SITE_A clinician  planning      2026-01-15       3 #> 4  PCAT_DEMO          R001  SITE_A clinician  planning      2026-01-15       4 #> 5  PCAT_DEMO          R001  SITE_A clinician  planning      2026-01-15       5 #> 6  PCAT_DEMO          R001  SITE_A clinician  planning      2026-01-15       6 #>   direction effect comment .pcat_row_error .pcat_row_warning .pcat_issue_codes #> 1         3      0      NA           FALSE             FALSE                   #> 2         3      0      NA           FALSE             FALSE                   #> 3         2     NA      NA           FALSE             FALSE                   #> 4         3      0      NA           FALSE             FALSE                   #> 5         2     NA      NA           FALSE             FALSE                   #> 6         2     NA      NA           FALSE             FALSE                   pcat_validation_issues(val) #> [1] .pcat_row  issue_code severity   message    #> <0 rows> (or 0-length row.names)

what: Export pCAT analysis results and profile figures — pcat_write_analysis
title: Export pCAT analysis results and profile figures — pcat_write_analysis
text: pcat_write_analysis() writes reproducible directory containing analysis manifest, validation findings, optional classified responses, item summaries, consensus diagnostics, optional action-plan worksheet, analysis settings, session information, optional profile PDF. pcat_save_profile_pdf() places one grouping combination page.

title: Export pCAT analysis results and profile figures — pcat_write_analysis

title: pcatR 1.0.0
text: Added pcat_analyse() structured pcat_analysis result object. Added pcat_write_analysis() reproducible export validation issues, classified responses, item summaries, consensus diagnostics, action plans, analysis manifest, optional multi-page profile PDF. Rebuilt visualization ggplot2, shared legends, readable facet labels, explicit neutral labels denominators, device-independent output. Improved profile defaults multi-panel readability expanded absent item records explicitly respondent change heatmaps. Added pcat_save_profile_pdf() place one grouping combination per page. Added original updated CFIR mapping metadata, including item 10’s secondary Funding mapping 2026 supplementary table. Added user-defined small-cell suppression pcat_summarise(). Clarified denominators: directional percentages use valid direction responses; five-category strength percentages use complete direction/effect responses. Added packaged technical user guide accessible pcat_user_guide(). Added GitHub Actions, pkgdown configuration, citation metadata, issue pull request templates, security/support/governance policies, release tools. Added automated tests pcat_self_test(). Added privacy-aware export controls, analysis settings/session provenance, deterministic cleanup stale package-generated output files. Preserved methodological boundary item display codes must summed purported validated total pCAT score.
